Miller Radio Control Flying Field for model aircraft (JC Parks)', 'Archery range with four shooting lanes and protective backdrops (JC Parks)']

attractions.name: Cole County Historical Society and Museum
attractions.description: The Cole County Historical Society and Museum is dedicated to preserving the records and artifacts of Cole County, Missouri. Located in a historic 1871 brick rowhouse built by Governor B. Gratz Brown, the museum features unique collections including inaugural gowns of Missouri's First Ladies and a comprehensive Civil War room. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.region: Cole County / Missouri State Capitol Historic District
attractions.hours: Office Hours: Tuesday 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM, Wednesday-Thursday 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Museum Tours: Thursday 12:00 PM - 2:30 PM (Other days by appointment); Library Hours: Monday 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM Official Website
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Saturday (Museum tours on Thursdays or by appointment) Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults (18+): $5, Seniors (60+): $4, Children (K-12): $3, Children (5 & under): Free, Members: Free Official Website
highlights: ["Collection of Missouri First Ladies' inaugural gowns", 'James Foster McHenry Civil War Room with mural and diorama', 'Historic B. Gratz Brown House (1871 Victorian rowhouse)', 'The Drawing Room with antique furnishings and gold-leaf mirrors', 'Extensive Research Library with local history and genealogical records', "Granny's Attic featuring toys and small historic objects"]
attractions.tips: The museum is closed annually from December 15 to February 1. Guided tours are typically available on Thursdays, but appointments can be made for other days by calling ahead. Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Dr. John Sappington Memorial Museum
attractions.description: The Dr. John Sappington Memorial Museum honors the 'Quinine Doctor' who pioneered the mass-marketing of anti-fever pills to treat malaria on the frontier. The museum features exhibits on 19th-century medical practices, Dr. Sappington's innovative business ventures, and his significant contributions to Missouri’s history and education.
attractions.hours: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily (March–November); 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM Friday–Sunday (December–February)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations accepted) Arrow Rock Village, Friends of Arrow Rock
highlights: ['Exhibits on the production and national marketing of Sappington’s Anti-Fever (quinine) pills', 'Original 19th-century medical tools and artifacts used by frontier physicians', 'Information on Dr. Sappington’s 1844 medical book, the first published west of the Mississippi', 'Display regarding the Sappington School Fund, which has aided thousands of Missouri students', 'Nearby original portraits of Dr. and Mrs. Sappington by famed artist George Caleb Bingham', 'The legend of Dr. Sappington’s coffin, which he kept under his bed and filled with treats for grandchildren']
attractions.tips: The museum is self-guided and located within the historic village of Arrow Rock. Visitors should wear comfortable walking shoes, as the town is a National Historic Landmark with many sites in walking distance. Cell service can be spotty, so it is recommended to download maps and visitor guides before arriving.
attractions.nearby: J. Huston Tavern, George Caleb Bingham House, Arrow Rock Lyceum Theatre, Sappington Cemetery State Historic Site, Big Muddy National Fish & Wildlife Refuge

attractions.name: Ellis-Porter Riverside Park
attractions.description: Ellis-Porter Riverside Park is a scenic 60-acre community park located on the historic east end of Jefferson City, Missouri. It features a variety of amenities including an aquatic center, an amphitheater hosting major concerts, sports complexes, and a picturesque overlook of the Missouri River. (JC Parks)

attractions.admission: Free for park entry; Pool Admission: Children (under 12 mos) Free, Youth (1-17) $7.50 before 5pm / $6.50 after 5pm, Adults (18+) $8.50 before 5pm / $7.50 after 5pm, Seniors (60+) and Military $5.50 (JC Parks)
highlights: 'Capital Region MU Health Care Amphitheater ([JC Parks)', 'Ellis-Porter Riverside Pool with drop-down slides and climbing walls (JC Parks)', 'Riverside Overlook with views of the Missouri River and Lewis and Clark kiosk (Visit Jefferson City)', 'Optimist Sports Complex for baseball, softball, and flag football (JC Parks)', 'Botanical Gardens maintained by the Jefferson City Master Gardeners (JC Parks)', 'Handball and Racquetball Courts (JC Parks)']
attractions.tips: Visit in the early morning or late afternoon for peaceful views of the sunrise or sunset over the Missouri River; parking is free with over 1,000 spots available on-site. (Evendo)

attractions.name: Honey Creek Access
attractions.description: Honey Creek Access is a Missouri Department of Conservation area located south of Jefferson City in Cole County. Covering 83.9 acres, it serves as a primary public access point for the Moreau River, featuring a concrete boat ramp and primitive camping sites. Visitors can enjoy a variety of outdoor activities, including fishing for catfish and bass, as well as archery deer and turkey hunting within its wooded riparian environment. (Missouri Department of Conservation)
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Tuesday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Wednesday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Thursday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Friday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Saturday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM*; Sunday: 4:00 AM - 10:00 PM* (*Hunting, fishing, trapping, dog training, camping, and boat launching allowed 24 hours daily) (Missouri Department of Conservation)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri Department of Conservation)
highlights: ['Concrete boat ramp providing primary access to the Moreau River.', 'Multi-species fishing opportunities, including catfish, black bass, and sunfish.', 'Designated primitive camping sites available for overnight stays.', 'Hunting opportunities for archery deer, turkey, rabbit, and squirrel.', 'Scenic riparian corridor perfect for nature observation and wildlife viewing.']
attractions.tips: Bank fishing is challenging due to the steep and heavily vegetated terrain; boating or kayaking is recommended for the best experience. While the area is open for general use from 4:00 AM to 10:00 PM, specific activities like fishing and hunting are allowed 24 hours daily. Portable tree stands are permitted from September 1 through January 31 but must be labeled and removed by February 1. (Missouri Department of Conservation)
attractions.nearby: Missouri State Capitol, Runge Conservation Nature Center, Scrivner Road Conservation Area, Moreau River (Missouri Department of Conservation)

attractions.description: The Jefferson City Convention and Visitors Bureau (JCCVB) is dedicated to marketing Jefferson City as a premier destination for tourism and conventions. The bureau provides comprehensive resources for visitors, including official guides, event calendars, and professional planning services to maximize the community's economic impact.

attractions.name: Jefferson Landing State Historic Site (Simonsen Access Area)
attractions.description: Jefferson Landing State Historic Site is a historic river landing and trade center established in the 1830s. It features the Lohman Building (1839), the city's oldest commercial structure, which once served as a grocery store, warehouse, and hotel for river travelers. The site reflects the significance of the Missouri River in the early development and commerce of the state's capital. (Missouri State Parks, Visit Jefferson City)
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri State Parks)
highlights: "Lohman Building (1839), the city's oldest commercial structure ([Missouri State Parks)", 'Christopher Maus House (1854) and Union Hotel (1855) (Missouri State Parks)', 'Missouri State Museum exhibits on river trade and transport (Missouri State Parks)', 'Scenic views of the Missouri River and the nearby state capitol (Visit Jefferson City)', 'Historic site of the original "Lohman\'s Landing," once owned by Ernest Simonsen (News Tribune)']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the museum in the Lohman Building to learn about the historic river commerce. It is a free and educational stop conveniently located near the Capitol. (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.nearby: Missouri State Capitol, Missouri State Museum, Governor's Mansion, and the Katy Trail (accessible via the nearby bridge) (Visit Jefferson City)

attractions.name: Jefferson Landing State Historic Site Missouri State Parks
attractions.description: Jefferson Landing State Historic Site is significant as a rare Missouri River landing that served as a bustling center of commerce during the mid-1800s. The site preserves historic structures like the 1839 Lohman Building and the 1855 Union Hotel, which were central to the capital's early transportation and trade. Today, it offers interactive exhibits on Missouri's cultural history and serves as a support facility for the nearby Missouri State Museum Missouri State Parks.
attractions.address: 100 Jefferson St, Jefferson City, MO 65101 Missouri State Parks
attractions.hours: Monday: 9 a.m. - 3 p.m., Tuesday: 9 a.m. - 3 p.m., Wednesday: 9 a.m. - 3 p.m., Thursday: 9 a.m. - 3 p.m., Friday: 9 a.m. - 3 p.m., Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Note: Temporarily closing June 1, 2026 - Oct 16, 2026) Missouri State Parks

attractions.tips: Reservations are required for all tours during school field trip seasons (March-May and October-November) and for groups larger than 10. Be aware of the upcoming temporary closure from June 1 to October 16, 2026. attractions.name: Katy Trail State Park
attractions.description: Katy Trail State Park is the longest developed rail-trail in the country, stretching 240 miles across Missouri along the former corridor of the Missouri-Kansas-Texas Railroad (MKT). In the Jefferson City area, the trail offers scenic views of the Missouri River and towering limestone bluffs, providing a peaceful and historic route for bicyclists, hikers, and equestrians (Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: Katy Road (West of US 54), Holts Summit, MO 65043 (North Jefferson Trailhead)
attractions.city: Jefferson City (North Jefferson / Holts Summit area)
attractions.region: Mid-Missouri / Jefferson City area (Visit Jefferson City)
attractions.hours: Daily: Sunrise to Sunset (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri State Parks, Airial Travel)
highlights: "The country's longest continuous developed rail-trail at 240 miles ([Wikipedia)", 'Scenic views of the Missouri River and majestic limestone bluffs (Missouri State Parks)', 'Part of the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail and the American Discovery Trail (Missouri State Parks)', 'Connected to downtown Jefferson City by a dedicated pedestrian/bike bridge with views of the State Capitol (Visit Jefferson City)', 'Features 26 trailheads and four fully restored historic railroad depots (Missouri State Parks)', 'Inducted into the Rail-Trail Hall of Fame in 2008 (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Water and restrooms at some trailheads are shut off from November through March. Access the heart of Jefferson City via the Missouri River Pedestrian Bridge from the North Jefferson Trailhead. Cell service can be spotty, so carrying a paper mileage map is highly recommended (Visit Missouri, Bike Katy Trail).
attractions.nearby: Missouri State Capitol, Missouri State Penitentiary, Carnahan Memorial Garden, North Jefferson Recreation Area (Visit Jefferson City, JC Parks)

attractions.name: Lake of the Ozarks State Park
attractions.description: Lake of the Ozarks State Park is Missouri's largest state park, encompassing over 17,000 acres and 85 miles of shoreline on the Grand Glaize Arm of the lake. It offers a diverse range of recreational activities, from exploring the underground wonders of Ozark Caverns to hiking 12 scenic trails and enjoying the only two public swimming beaches on the lake. (Missouri State Parks, Visit Missouri)
attractions.region: Camden and Miller counties, Jefferson City area (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Sunrise to one-half hour after sunset; Park Office: 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Mon-Fri; Beaches: Sunrise to sunset (Seasonally May 22–Sept. 7); Ozark Caverns: Varied seasonal hours (e.g., 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily in peak summer) (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Year-round (grounds); Seasonal (beaches and caverns) (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Park Entrance: Free (Tripadvisor); Ozark Caverns Tours: Adults $13, Teenagers (13-17) $10, Children (6-12) $8, Children under 6 Free (Missouri State Parks); Boat Launch: $5-$7 (StateParks.com)
highlights: "Missouri's largest state park spanning 17,441 acres ([Missouri State Parks)", '85 miles of shoreline for fishing, boating, and swimming (Visit Missouri)', "Ozark Caverns featuring the unique 'Angel Showers' waterfall formation (Missouri State Parks)", 'Two sandy swimming beaches, the only public beaches on the Lake of the Ozarks (Funlake)', '12 diverse trails for hikers, backpackers, equestrians, and bicyclists (Wikipedia)', 'A unique aquatic trail designed for boaters with marked interpretive stops (Funlake)']
attractions.tips: Water shoes are highly recommended as the lake bottom is rocky; the lake water may stain light-colored swimsuits. It is advisable to reserve cave tours in advance during the summer months, and visitors should be vigilant about ticks and chiggers when hiking wooded trails. (Kansas City Mom Collective, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Ha Ha Tonka State Park, Bridal Cave, Bagnell Dam, Osage Beach Outlet Marketplace, Stark Caverns (Funlake, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Missouri Governor's Mansion
attractions.description: The Missouri Governor’s Mansion has served as the official residence for the state's executives since its completion in 1871. Situated on 10 acres overlooking the Missouri River and the State Capitol, this historic three-story residence is an iconic example of Second Empire architecture and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Mansion.mo.gov, Friends of the Missouri Governor's Mansion
attractions.region: Missouri State Capitol Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM & 12:30 PM – 2:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM & 12:30 PM – 2:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM & 12:30 PM – 2:00 PM (March–June; last tour 15 minutes before closing) Missouri State Parks, Friends of the Missouri Governor's Mansion
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Thursday (March–June; excluding first week of June); Candlelight Tours in December Missouri State Parks, Friends of the Missouri Governor's Mansion
attractions.admission: Free (reservations required 48 hours in advance) Missouri State Parks, Mansion.mo.gov

attractions.tips: Tours must be reserved at least 48 hours in advance and confirmed before arrival. Visitors should bring a photo ID for security purposes and plan for approximately 15 minutes for the tour itself, though extra time is recommended if visiting the nearby Capitol. Completed in 1917, the building houses the Missouri State Museum and features a world-renowned collection of murals, including major works by Thomas Hart Benton Missouri State Parks, Visit Jefferson City.

attractions.name: Missouri State Highway Patrol Water Patrol Division
attractions.description: The Missouri State Highway Patrol Water Patrol Division is responsible for enforcing boating laws and ensuring water safety across the state's lakes and rivers. Headquartered in Jefferson City, the division provides essential services such as boater safety education, accident investigation, and marine enforcement to promote a secure environment for all recreationists on Missouri's waterways. (Missouri State Highway Patrol)
attractions.address: 1510 East Elm Street, Jefferson City, MO 65102
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Missouri Automated Criminal History System, Visit Jefferson City)

attractions.description: The Missouri State Museum is located on the first floor of the Missouri State Capitol and serves as a premier destination for exploring the state's natural and cultural history (mostateparks.com). The museum features two primary galleries, History Hall and Resources Hall, which house an extensive collection of artifacts including historic military flags and exhibits on Missouri trailblazers (Wikipedia, mostateparks.com).
attractions.address: 201 W Capitol Ave, Jefferson City, MO 65101
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday & State Holidays: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (mostateparks.com)

attractions.admission: Free (mostateparks.com, Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'History Hall (formerly Soldiers and Sailors Memorial Hall) ([mostateparks.com)', 'Resources Hall (showcasing state products and natural resources) (mostateparks.com)', 'Missouri Bicentennial Timeline exhibit (mostateparks.com)', 'Missouri Trailblazers gallery (mostateparks.com)', 'Missouri Veterans Gallery (mostateparks.com)', "'Memory in Cloth' collection of over 400 military flags (mostateparks.com)"]
attractions.tips: Free guided tours of the State Capitol are provided by museum staff daily, but reservations are highly recommended and can be made through the museum's online system (mostateparks.com). attractions.name: Missouri State Penitentiary (Official Website)
attractions.description: Operating for 168 years, the Missouri State Penitentiary was once the oldest continually operating prison west of the Mississippi River and was infamously dubbed "the bloodiest 47 acres in America" by Time Magazine (Tripadvisor). Today, it serves as a historic landmark offering immersive guided history and ghost tours through preserved cellblocks and the prison gas chamber (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Museum: Mon–Fri 10:00 am – 4:00 pm, Sat 10:00 am – 3:00 pm; Tours: Vary by booking (March–November) (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: March through November for tours; Museum open year-round Monday–Saturday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Museum: $2 (Free with prison tour). Tours: 2-Hour History ($30), 3-Hour In-Depth History ($40), 3-Hour Former Inmate ($40), 2-Hour Ghost ($40), 3-Hour Ghost Hunt ($50), 5-Hour Overnight Paranormal ($90), 8-Hour Overnight Paranormal ($115) (Singenuity Booking)
highlights: 'Housing Unit 4 (Dungeon Cells and Upper Walks) ([Official Website)', 'The Prison Gas Chamber (Official Website)', 'Housing Unit 1 (Control Center) (Official Website)', 'Centennial Cells (Official Website)', 'Housing Unit 3 (General Population) (Official Website)', 'Missouri State Penitentiary Museum (Marmaduke House) (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Dress for the weather as there is no heat or AC; wear comfortable, closed-toe shoes for extensive walking; advance reservations are highly recommended as tours often sell out; all visitors must sign a digital waiver (Official Website).

attractions.admission: Free (Public campus space)
highlights: ['The Columns (remnants of Academic Hall)', 'Jesse Hall (main administration building with white dome)', "Thomas Jefferson's original tombstone", 'Confucius Stone Lions (gift from China)', 'David R. Francis statue (tradition of rubbing the nose for good luck)', "The Residence on the Quad (Chancellor's Residence, built in 1867)"]
attractions.tips: Visit during the 'golden hours' of sunrise or sunset for the best photos of the Columns. Rub the nose of the David R. Francis statue before an exam for good luck, a long-standing student tradition. The area is very walkable and often hosts campus events like drumline performances.
attractions.nearby: Museum of Art and Archaeology, Downtown Columbia, Mizzou Student Center, Peace Park, Stephens Lake Park

attractions.name: Deutschheim State Historic Site
attractions.description: Deutschheim State Historic Site preserves several structures dating from 1840 to 1890 that reflect the period when German immigrants first settled the Missouri River valley. Through guided tours of historic houses and gardens, visitors can experience the daily life, traditions, and significant contributions of 19th-century German-Americans (Missouri State Parks, Visit Hermann).
attractions.region: Hermann Historic District, Missouri River Valley (TCLF, Missouri State Parks)
attractions.hours: On-Season (April 1–Oct. 31): 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. daily. Off-Season (Nov. 1–March 31): 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. Thursday–Sunday. Tours offered at 10:00 a.m., 12:30 p.m., and 2:30 p.m. (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Daily (April 1–Oct. 31); Thursday through Sunday (Nov. 1–March 31) (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Adults (18+): $5, Youth (6-17): $3, Children (0-5): Free with paid adult. Group rates: Adults $4, Youth $2.50. Visitor Center: Free. (Missouri State Parks, Experience Hermann)
highlights: 'Pommer-Gentner House, a substantial 1840 German Neoclassical brick home ([Missouri State Parks)', 'Carl Strehly House and winery featuring a timber-frame structure and historic wine cellar (Missouri State Parks)', 'Traditional German four-square kitchen gardens with heirloom plants (Visit Hermann)', 'A working Washington Press used to print the first German newspaper west of the Mississippi (Visit Hermann)', 'Original Pommer pianos, with only four known to still exist (Lagom Adventures)', 'Historic grapevines over 150 years old, including original Norton grapes (Lagom Adventures)']
attractions.tips: Advance reservations for tours are highly recommended and can be made online or by phone. The free visitor center includes a gift shop featuring traditional German cookie molds and baking supplies (Missouri State Parks, Expedia).
attractions.nearby: Stone Hill Winery, Hermannhof Winery, Historic Hermann Museum, Hermann Farm, Katy Trail, and the Hermann Wine Trail (Visit Hermann, Hermann Wurst Haus)

attractions.name: Faurot Field at Memorial Stadium
attractions.description: Faurot Field at Memorial Stadium is the home of the University of Missouri Tigers football program and a central landmark of the Columbia community. Originally opened in 1926, the stadium is famous for its iconic "Rock M" berm in the north end zone and its storied atmosphere known as "The Zou."
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Athletics Office); Stadium open for scheduled events and game days (University of Missouri Athletics)
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (Athletics Office); Game Days and Event Days (Stadium)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; 2026 single-game tickets range from approximately $38 (Grass Berm) to over $200 (Premium seating). Tiers include Student, Grass Berm General Admission, Reserved Seating, Tiger Deck, and Walsworth Family Columns Club.
highlights: 'Home of the Missouri Tigers football team since 1926 ([University of Missouri Athletics)', 'Features the iconic "Rock M" stone berm in the north end zone (Wikipedia)', 'Capacity of approximately 62,621 with a record attendance of over 75,000 (Wikipedia)', 'Home to the "MIZ-ZOU" chant and deep-rooted collegiate traditions (Wikipedia)', 'Undergoing the Memorial Stadium Centennial Project to commemorate its 100th anniversary (University of Missouri Athletics)', 'Includes luxury amenities like the Walsworth Family Columns Club and the Tiger Deck (University of Missouri Athletics)']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to witness the 'Rock M' traditions and participate in the stadium-wide 'MIZ-ZOU' chant. Use the Tiger Line shuttle for convenient game-day parking and avoid the heavy traffic near Stadium Boulevard. (Parking & Transportation)
attractions.nearby: Hearnes Center, Mizzou Arena, Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, The District (Downtown Columbia), Logboat Brewing Co. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Finger Lakes State Park
attractions.description: Finger Lakes State Park is a 1,128-acre reclaimed coal strip-mining site that now serves as a premier destination for off-road motorcycling and ATV riding. The park features a rugged landscape with steep hills and deep ravines, as well as several finger-shaped lakes joined by canals for water recreation. Missouri State Parks
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Apr–Oct 6 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily; Nov–Mar 6 a.m. to 9 p.m. daily. Park Office: Summer (Apr–Oct) 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. daily; Winter (Nov–Mar) 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. daily. Motocross/ORV Area: 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. or sunset daily. Beach: Sunrise to sunset (May 22 to Sept. 7). Missouri State Parks
attractions.admission: Free admission to park grounds; ORV permits: $6 per vehicle, per day Missouri State Parks; Kayak/Canoe rentals: $10-15 for two-hour minimum, $25-30 per day Waymarking
highlights: ['900 acres of off-road motorcycle and ATV trails with steep hills and deep ravines', 'Seven finger-shaped lakes joined by canals for a 4.5-mile water trail used for kayaking and canoeing', 'Professional-style motocross track and a dedicated youth riding area for younger enthusiasts', 'Challenging 3.8-mile Kelley Branch Mountain Bike Trail and a specialized pump track for bikers', 'Sand swimming beach with a change house open during the summer season', 'Scuba diving and fishing for bass, bluegill, crappie, and catfish in the various strip-pits']
attractions.tips: ORV permits must be purchased online or at the office before riding. All ATVs and side-by-sides must be equipped with an orange flag and operators must wear helmets. Scuba divers must be certified and should check in at the park office. Missouri State Parks
attractions.nearby: Rocky Fork Lakes Conservation Area, Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, Rudolf Bennitt State Wildlife Area, Hunt Brothers Pizza Tripadvisor, StateParks.com

attractions.description: Flat Branch Pub & Brewing opened in 1994 as Columbia's first brewery since 1841, located in a historic 1927 brick warehouse that formerly served as a car dealership. The establishment offers a diverse menu alongside award-winning hand-crafted beers on tap in an upscale industrial setting or on its spacious outdoor patio. (Official Website)

attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)

attractions.tips: Flat Branch does not accept reservations, so arriving early is recommended to avoid long waits, especially during peak times and game days. The patio is a highly recommended spot on nice days, and visitors should try the signature Green Chili Beer or local favorites like the Oil Change Oatmeal Stout. (Official Website, Reddit, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Flat Branch Park, Broadway Diner, Shakespeare's Pizza, The Blue Note, The District (Downtown Columbia) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fulton State Hospital
attractions.description: Fulton State Hospital is the oldest public mental health facility west of the Mississippi River, having opened its doors in 1851. It serves as a major treatment and forensic center for the state of Missouri, providing high-security psychiatric care and specialized rehabilitation services. The campus features the modern Nixon Forensic Center and specialized programs for individuals with developmental disabilities and sexually violent predators. Missouri Department of Mental Health

attractions.name: Graham Cave State Park
attractions.description: Graham Cave State Park is a 386-acre public recreation area located in Montgomery County, Missouri, known for its significant archaeological importance. The park's centerpiece, Graham Cave, provided shelter to Native Americans 8,000 to 10,000 years ago and was the first archaeological site in the United States to be designated a National Historic Landmark. Visitors can explore various trails, enjoy boating on the Loutre River, or camp in the park’s quiet, wooded setting. (Wikipedia, Missouri State Parks)
attractions.hours: Park Grounds & Boat Launch: 7 a.m. to sunset daily, year-round. Visitor Center (April-October): 10 a.m. to 3 p.m., Thursday through Saturday (Closed Sunday-Wednesday). Visitor Center (November-March): Closed. (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Park Grounds); Thursday-Saturday (Visitor Center, April-October) (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (that away dad)
highlights: ['Graham Cave (Prehistoric archaeological site)', 'Graham Cave Glades Natural Area (Sandstone and dolomite glades)', '4.6 miles of scenic hiking trails', 'Loutre River boat ramp for boating and fishing', 'Wooded campground with modern restrooms', 'Picnic areas and playground facilities']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy shoes for hiking and bring plenty of water, sunscreen, and insect repellent. Be sure to check for ticks after exploring the trails. Plan your visit between Thursday and Saturday if you wish to see the Visitor Center during the on-season. (Missouri Department of Natural Resources, Missouri State Parks)

attractions.name: Grand Glaize Beach (Public Beach #2)
attractions.description: Grand Glaize Beach, also known as Public Beach #2, is a popular swimming area within Lake of the Ozarks State Park. It features a sandy beach, shaded picnic areas, and proximity to a marina offering boat rentals and supplies. (Missouri State Parks, FunLake)

attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Beach Season: May 22 to Sept. 7) (Missouri State Parks)

attractions.tips: Lifeguards are not provided; swim at your own risk. No pets are allowed on the beach area. Reserve picnic shelters in advance for a fee to guarantee availability. (Missouri State Parks, FunLake)

attractions.name: Grindstone Nature Area
attractions.description: Grindstone Nature Area is a 199-acre city park in southeast Columbia, Missouri, featuring a diverse landscape of woods, open fields, bluffs, and the winding Grindstone Creek. The area serves as a significant trailhead for both the Hinkson Creek and Grindstone Creek trails, and is particularly popular for its extensive leash-free area for dogs. It also contains the Gordon Tract Archeological Site, which is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. (City of Columbia, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Daily, 6 a.m. to 11 p.m. (City of Columbia)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Columbia)
highlights: '199 acres of diverse natural landscapes including woods, prairie, and bluffs ([City of Columbia)', 'Large leash-free area for dogs to roam across approximately 200 acres (City of Columbia)', 'Extensive trail network with over 5 miles of dirt nature trails and 6 miles of multi-use trails (City of Columbia)', 'Historic landmarks including a concrete silo and the remains of a stone spring house (City of Columbia)', 'Home to the Gordon Tract Archeological Site, a National Register of Historic Places listing (Wikipedia)', 'Scenic views of wildflowers and native prairie grasses (City of Columbia)']
attractions.tips: Dogs are allowed off-leash in most areas but must be leashed on the Hinkson Creek Trail and near the parking lot. The dirt nature trails can become muddy after rain, so sturdy hiking shoes are recommended. Parking is available at the main lot off Old 63 South or via Rock Quarry Road at Capen Park. (City of Columbia, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Capen Park, Hinkson Creek Trail, Stephens Lake Park, Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, University of Missouri Campus (City of Columbia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Ha Ha Tonka State Park
attractions.description: Ha Ha Tonka State Park is a premier geological wonderland featuring sinkholes, caves, a massive natural bridge, and Missouri’s 12th-largest spring. The park is most famous for the ruins of a turn-of-the-century stone castle that overlooks the Lake of the Ozarks from high atop a sheer bluff (Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Camden County / Lake of the Ozarks area
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Apr-Oct: 7 a.m.–Sunset Daily; Nov-Mar: 8 a.m.–Sunset Daily. Visitor Center: Apr-Oct: 10 a.m.–5 p.m. Daily; Nov-Mar: Not regularly staffed (Missouri State Parks).
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri State Parks, VisitMO)
highlights: 'Castle Ruins: The remains of a 20th-century European-style stone mansion built by Robert McClure Snyder ([Missouri State Parks).', 'Ha Ha Tonka Spring: A vibrant turquoise spring discharging over 48 million gallons of water daily (Point Randall Resort).', 'Natural Bridge: A massive 60-foot geologic formation that serves as a primary entrance to the castle grounds (Point Randall Resort).', "Karst Features: Unique geological formations including caves like Devil's Kitchen and numerous sinkholes (Wikipedia).", 'Hiking Trails: Over 15 miles of winding trails and boardwalks, including a challenging 316-step staircase to the ruins (VisitMO).', 'Lake Views: Majestic panoramas of the Niangua arm of the Lake of the Ozarks from towering bluffs (VisitMO).']
attractions.tips: Pack plenty of water and snacks as amenities are limited beyond the visitor center area. Wear sturdy shoes for rugged trails and the steep climb to the castle. Pets are allowed on leashes, and the Spring Trail boardwalk is recommended for an accessible view of the crystal-clear waters (The Wandering Whittens, Point Randall Resort).
attractions.nearby: Lake of the Ozarks State Park, Bridal Cave, Camden County Museum, Niangua Arm of the Lake of the Ozarks (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Hartsburg Pumpkin Festival
attractions.description: The Hartsburg Pumpkin Festival is a long-standing tradition in mid-Missouri that celebrates the region's rich agricultural heritage. Known as "Missouri's Pumpkin Patch," this small-town event draws tens of thousands of visitors for a weekend of community, crafts, and family-friendly entertainment in the fertile Missouri River bottom. Official Website

attractions.tips: Arrive early to navigate the heavy traffic and secure parking, which typically costs $5 and supports the local FFA program. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the festival spans the town's compact, historic streets. Consider using the shuttle services provided from nearby schools if available to avoid downtown congestion. Official Website FAQ, Boone County Journal

attractions.name: Hermann Missouri Wine Country
attractions.description: Hermann is a historic German village tucked away in the Missouri River Valley, widely recognized as the heart of Missouri Wine Country. The region features several award-winning, family-owned wineries that offer daily tastings, cellar tours, and stunning views of rolling vineyards and the Missouri River. Visit Hermann, Hermann Wine Trail

attractions.hours: Wineries: Mon-Thu 10:00 am - 5:00 pm, Fri-Sat 10:00 am - 5:30 pm, Sun 11:00 am - 4:00 pm (times vary by location); Visitor Center: Daily 8:00 am - 8:00 pm. Visit Hermann

Founded in 1960, the society operates a museum in downtown Fulton's historic Brick District and a dedicated research center for genealogical and archival studies. It offers a wide range of educational programs, rotating exhibits, and community events to engage all generations in local history (Kingdom of Callaway Historical Society).

attractions.description: Klondike Park is a scenic 250-acre property situated on the bluffs of the Missouri River, formerly serving as a silica sand quarry. The park features unique white silica sand beaches, a fishing lake, and over four miles of multi-use trails offering spectacular views of the river valley. It is also home to The Creative at Klondike, an arts center that provides gallery space and community programs. (Official Website, Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Maries County Historical Society Museum (Old Jail Museum Complex)
attractions.description: The Maries County Historical Society operates a five-building museum complex that preserves the rich pioneer and civil history of the region. Visitors can explore historic structures including an 1850s limestone jail, log houses, and a dedicated history building filled with relics ranging from mastodon bones to Civil War artifacts. (Maries County Historical Society)
attractions.address: 5th & Mill Streets, Vienna, MO 65582 (Museum Complex); PO Box 289, Vienna, MO 65582 (Mailing)
attractions.hours: Museum Complex: Sundays 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (typically June–October); Research Room (at Courthouse): Wednesdays 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Waymarking, Maries County Historical Society)
attractions.admission: Free for research; Museum tours by donation or appointment (Waymarking, Maries County Historical Society)
highlights: ['Old Jail and Sheriff’s Quarter (built 1856-1858) featuring prisoner graffiti and Civil War relics', 'Felker House, a two-story log home demonstrating mid-1800s settler life', 'Latham House, recognized as the first home built in Vienna (circa 1855)', 'Maries County Building housing artifacts from the Old Vichy Post Office and first Maries County Bank', 'Hollenbeck House containing furniture and keepsakes from prominent local citizens and orphan train memorabilia', '10,000-year-old mastodon bones and extensive arrowhead collections']

attractions.name: Mark Twain State Park
attractions.description: Nestled in the Salt River Hills of north-central Missouri, Mark Twain State Park offers visitors access to the expansive Mark Twain Lake and a variety of outdoor recreational activities. The park features scenic bluffs, diverse wildlife such as bald eagles and white-tailed deer, and historic structures like the CCC-built Buzzard's Roost picnic shelter (Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia).

attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Sunrise to 10 p.m. daily; Beach (seasonal): Sunrise to sunset; Park Office (Apr-Oct): Tue-Sat 10 a.m.-4 p.m., Closed Sun-Mon; Park Office (Nov-Mar): Fri-Sat 10 a.m.-4 p.m., Closed Sun-Thu (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free entry to the park; fees apply for camping and at the adjacent Mark Twain Birthplace State Historic Site museum (Facebook, stateparks.com)
highlights: 'Access to the 18,600-acre Mark Twain Lake for boating and fishing ([Missouri State Parks)', 'Over six miles of hiking trails with lake views and oak-hickory forests (Missouri State Parks)', "Historic Buzzard's Roost picnic shelter, built by the CCC and listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Wikipedia)", 'Opportunities for camping with basic and electric sites available (Missouri State Parks)', 'Wildlife viewing including white-tailed deer, turkeys, and nesting bald eagles (Missouri State Parks)', 'Adjacent to the Mark Twain Birthplace State Historic Site (Missouri State Parks)']
attractions.tips: Check the official Park and Site Status Map for any temporary closures or advisories before your visit. Pets are welcome in the park but are strictly prohibited on the public beach area. For the best wildlife viewing, visit in the winter months to spot bald eagles nesting or soaring over the lake (Missouri State Parks, Michael Shepherd Jordan).

The building features a majestic 143-foot tower inspired by Oxford's Magdalen College and houses student offices, meeting rooms, and various dining options. (unions.missouri.edu, Show Me Mizzou)

Green Chapel, a nondenominational space used for reflection and special services. (The Maneater)', 'Social and study hubs including the Bengal Lair and Tiger Terrace. attractions.description: The Missouri River Bird Observatory (MRBO) is a science-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to conserving birds and their habitats through research, education, and advocacy. Founded in 2010, the organization monitors avian populations across nearly 100,000 acres of Missouri's most imperiled prairies, wetlands, and forests to inform conservation policy and land management.

attractions.name: Missouri–Kansas–Texas Bridge (Katy Bridge)
attractions.description: The Missouri–Kansas–Texas (MKT) Bridge, commonly known as the Katy Bridge, is a historic vertical lift rail bridge spanning the Missouri River. Built in 1932, it served the MKT Railroad for over 50 years and is now being rehabilitated into a pedestrian and bicycle parkway as part of the Katy Trail State Park. Visitors can walk out onto the restored southern portion of the bridge for sweeping views of the river and local railroad activity.
attractions.address: Entrance near 100 East Spring Street (via Isle of Capri Casino parking lot)
attractions.hours: Open daily, year-round (Typically dawn to dusk)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Historic 408-foot vertical lift span, once the longest of its kind in the U.S.', 'A key landmark along the 240-mile Katy Trail State Park', 'Restored pedestrian and bike path on the southern section of the bridge', 'Scenic overlooks of the Missouri River and the surrounding Boonslick region', "Located in the heart of Boonville's historic Depot District", "Maintained by the Katy Bridge Coalition to preserve Missouri's rail history"]
attractions.tips: Access the bridge by parking in the lot behind the Isle of Capri Casino and walking up the hill. The lift span is currently locked in the raised position, so you can only walk about halfway across the river from the Boonville side. For a complete river crossing, use the nearby Boonslick Bridge (Highway 40) which features a dedicated pedestrian walkway.
attractions.nearby: Katy Depot, Rivers, Rails & Trails Museum, Isle of Capri Casino, Harley Park, Hotel Frederick

attractions.description: Ozark Caverns is an unlit, state-owned cave within Lake of the Ozarks State Park, offering a natural and intimate underground experience. It is famous for the "Angel Showers" formation, where water constantly drips from the ceiling, and features historic bear claw marks and diverse cave wildlife (Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Camden County, Lake of the Ozarks Region (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.hours: May 15-31: Sat-Sun 11am, 1pm, 3pm; June 1-Aug 15: Daily 11am, 12:30pm, 2pm, 4pm; Aug 16-31: Tue-Sat 12:30pm, 2pm; Sept 1-15: Sat-Sun 11am, 1pm, 3pm; Sept 16-May 14: Closed (Missouri State Parks).
attractions.admission: Adult: $13, Teenager (13-17): $10, Child (6-12): $8, Child (under 6): Free. Group rates: Adult/Teen $8, Child $6 (Missouri State Parks).

attractions.tips: The cave remains a constant 56°F year-round, so a light jacket is recommended. Items like purses, backpacks, and strollers are prohibited inside the cave to protect bats from White Nose Syndrome (Missouri State Parks).

attractions.name: Rock Bridge Memorial State Park
attractions.description: Rock Bridge Memorial State Park encompasses 2,273 acres and is celebrated for its remarkable karst topography, including its namesake natural rock bridge and the cool depths of the Devil's Icebox. Visitors can enjoy over 27 miles of diverse trails for hiking, biking, and horseback riding, while also exploring caves and sinkholes that offer a glimpse into Missouri's unique geologic underworld. (Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Sunrise to sunset daily (Park Grounds); Office: 9 a.m. to 3 p.m., Monday through Friday. (Missouri State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri State Parks, Expedia)
highlights: 'Natural Rock Bridge: A massive stone bridge formed by the collapse of a cave system.', "Devil's Icebox: A double sinkhole with an underground stream that maintains a year-round temperature of 56°F.", "Connor's Cave: A 166-foot cave downstream from the bridge that is open for self-guided exploration.", 'Gans Creek Wild Area: A pristine area with over 8 miles of trails and impressive bluffs.', 'Extensive Trail System: Over 27 miles of trails for hiking, mountain biking, and equestrian use. ([Missouri State Parks, Visit MO)']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy shoes you don't mind getting wet for cave exploration. Be prepared for many stairs on the Devil's Icebox boardwalk trail. Note that Devil's Icebox Cave (beyond the entrance) is closed to protect local bat populations. Check for trail closures after rain as many are sensitive to muddy conditions. (Tripadvisor, Visit MO)

attractions.name: Stephens Lake Park (City of Columbia)
attractions.description: Stephens Lake Park is a 116-acre destination known as the "crown jewel" of Columbia's park system, featuring an 11-acre lake for fishing and swimming. It serves as an accredited Level II arboretum and is a central hub for community festivals, outdoor concerts, and winter sports like sledding and ice skating. The park offers diverse amenities including a swimming beach, spraygrounds, and extensive walking trails (City of Columbia).
attractions.address: 2001 E Broadway, Columbia, MO 65201 (City of Columbia)
attractions.hours: 6 a.m. to 11 p.m. daily; Fishing hours: 4:30 a.m. to 11 p.m. (City of Columbia)
attractions.admission: Free (Love Columbia)
highlights: '11-acre lake with a swimming beach and fishing areas ([City of Columbia)', 'Level II accredited arboretum with diverse tree collections (ArbNet)', 'Outdoor amphitheater for community concerts and events (City of Columbia)', 'Seasonal spraygrounds and waterfalls for summer recreation (Love Columbia)', 'Comprehensive trail system including a 0.6-mile lake trail and a 1.7-mile perimeter trail (City of Columbia)', 'Dedicated sled hill and ice skating areas for winter sports (Visit CoMo)']
attractions.tips: Swimming is allowed only on the west side of the boardwalk, while non-motorized boating is permitted on the east side. Check the city's website for ice thickness updates before skating in winter, and note that most restrooms are closed seasonally from October 15 to April 15. Free WiFi is accessible near the shelters and beach area (City of Columbia).
attractions.nearby: Stephens College, University of Missouri, Boone Hospital, Logboat Brewing Company, and Lions-Stephens Park (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Stone Hill Winery
attractions.description: Established in 1847 by German immigrants, Stone Hill Winery is Missouri’s oldest and most awarded winery, perched prominently on a hill with commanding views of the town of Hermann. It is home to the largest series of arched underground cellars in the United States, offering visitors a unique look into the state's deep winemaking heritage. Today, it remains a premier destination where guests can enjoy world-class wines, historic cellar tours, and German-inspired cuisine at the Vintage 1847 Restaurant. Stone Hill Winery, Hermann Hill
attractions.hours: Sales Room: Mon-Thu: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Fri-Sat: 10:00 AM - 5:30 PM, Sun: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Vintage 1847 Restaurant: Mon: 11:30 AM - 8:00 PM, Tue-Wed: Closed, Thu-Sat: 11:30 AM - 8:00 PM, Sun: 11:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Sherry House Pavilion: Fri: 5:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Sat: 11:00 AM - 5:30 PM Stone Hill Winery
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed: Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day) Stone Hill Winery
attractions.admission: Winery Tour: Free, Museum Room: Free, Wine Tasting: $15 (includes souvenir glass and 6 samples), Group Outings: Pricing upon request Stone Hill Winery
highlights: 'Home to the largest series of arched underground cellars in the United States. [Stone Hill Winery', "Missouri's oldest and most awarded winery, established in 1847. Wikipedia", 'Spectacular hilltop views overlooking the historic town of Hermann and the Missouri River Valley. Hermann Hill', 'Vintage 1847 Restaurant, located in the original stable and carriage house, serving German and American cuisine. Stone Hill Winery', 'Free guided tours of the historic underground cellars and a self-guided Museum Room. Stone Hill Winery', 'Producer of award-winning wines, including the world-renowned Norton. Stone Hill Winery']
attractions.tips: Take the free underground cellar tour to learn about the winery's history and the Prohibition era. Pets are welcome on the grounds but must be on a leash and are not allowed inside the tasting room. Arrive early for tastings as they are first-come, first-served with no reservations. You can also enjoy a picnic on the hillside with wine and snacks from their charcuterie fridge. Stone Hill Winery, Rent Branson
attractions.nearby: Adam Puchta Winery, Hermannhof Winery, Deutschheim State Historic Site, Hermann Riverfront Park, and the Katy Trail. Tripadvisor, Visit Hermann

attractions.name: The Blue Note (The Blue Note)
attractions.description: The Blue Note is a premier live music venue and historic theater located in the heart of downtown Columbia, Missouri. Established in 1980, it is housed in the former Varsity Theater building and has hosted a vast array of legendary national and local acts. Beyond concerts, the venue also features stand-up comedy, dance parties, and community events (The Blue Note About Us, Wikipedia))
attractions.address: 17 N. 9th Street, Columbia, MO 65201 (The Blue Note)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Fridays 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Event Days: Box office opens 30 minutes prior to doors until the headlining act begins (The Blue Note Contact)
attractions.days_open: Open for scheduled events throughout the week; box office is open on Fridays (The Blue Note)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically ranges from $15 to $60+; specific prices for all tiers are available on the event calendar (The Blue Note Calendar)
highlights: 'Historic 1927 theater building originally known as The Varsity Theater ([The Blue Note)', 'Contributing property to the North Ninth Street Historic District (Wikipedia))', 'Has hosted legendary artists including Johnny Cash, Willie Nelson, and the Red Hot Chili Peppers (The Blue Note)', 'Features a versatile multi-level space with a tiered main floor and upper balcony with opera boxes (The Blue Note)', 'Known for unique events like "Brew n\' View" movie screenings and summer outdoor concerts on 9th Street (Gigseekr)']
attractions.tips: Buy tickets in person at the box office on Fridays to avoid online service fees. Arrive early for popular shows as most tickets are general admission and standing room only. Always check the specific age restriction for each show, as they vary between all-ages, 18+, and 21+ (The Blue Note FAQ)
attractions.nearby: Rose Music Hall, The Tiger Hotel, University of Missouri, Stephens College, Ninth Street Public House (Lewis and Clark Trail Experience)

attractions.name: The Lodge of Four Seasons
attractions.description: The Lodge of Four Seasons is an iconic lakeside resort situated on the edge of Lake of the Ozarks, Missouri. Since 1964, it has offered a blend of warm hospitality, 54 holes of championship golf, and a world-class spa across its 350-acre property (Lodge of Four Seasons).

attractions.tips: Book spa treatments and tee times in advance, especially during peak season. Guests dining at resort restaurants can enjoy free docking at the marina. Check the online event calendar for seasonal activities like sunrise yoga or outdoor movie nights (Lodge of Four Seasons, Lodge of Four Seasons, Lodge of Four Seasons).

attractions.name: Thespian Hall
attractions.description: Thespian Hall is the oldest theater still in use west of the Alleghenies and has been a cornerstone of Boonville's cultural life since its construction in 1855-1857. Over its long history, it has served many roles, including a theater, a Civil War hospital, and a community center, and it is now listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
attractions.hours: Office Hours: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri: 10:00am – 4:00pm; Wed: Closed. Tours of Thespian Hall are by appointment only.
attractions.days_open: Tours by appointment only; events as scheduled; office open Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri.
attractions.admission: Group tours by appointment only: $10.00 per person, $5.00 per person if under 18. Festival ticket prices vary (e.g., Big Muddy Folk Festival: $35/evening, $60/weekend).
highlights: ['Oldest theater still in use west of the Alleghenies (circa 1856-1857).', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1969.', 'Home to the Missouri River Festival of the Arts, the oldest performing arts festival in Missouri.', 'Hosts the annual Big Muddy Folk Festival every April.', 'Served as a hospital and barracks during the American Civil War.', 'Operated by the Friends of Historic Boonville, a non-profit dedicated to preservation.']
attractions.tips: Tours must be scheduled in advance through the Friends of Historic Boonville office. Visit during the Missouri River Festival of the Arts (August) or the Big Muddy Folk Festival (April) for the best cultural experience.
attractions.nearby: Old Cooper County Jail & Hanging Barn, Hain House & Memorial Gardens, Katy Trail State Park, River Rails and Trails Museum, Isle of Capri Casino.

attractions.name: University of Missouri (Mizzou)
attractions.description: The University of Missouri, founded in 1839, is a public land-grant research university and the flagship of the University of Missouri System. The 1,262-acre campus is officially designated as the Mizzou Botanic Garden and is celebrated for its historic neo-Gothic and red-brick architecture. It features iconic landmarks like the Francis Quadrangle and the six granite Columns, which remain from the university's original academic hall. (University of Missouri, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Visitor Relations: Mon–Fri 8 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Campus: Open 24/7 (University of Missouri)
attractions.admission: Free (Mizzou Admissions)
highlights: ['Francis Quadrangle and its historic granite Columns', 'Jesse Hall, featuring its iconic dome and Jesse Auditorium', 'Mizzou Botanic Garden with over 11,000 trees and themed plantings', 'Memorial Union, a stunning neo-Gothic tribute to fallen soldiers', 'The Missouri School of Journalism, the world’s first of its kind', "MizzouRec, consistently ranked as one of the nation's top recreation centers"]
attractions.tips: Visitors should purchase a parking permit or use metered parking as enforcement is strict from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. on weekdays. Guided walking tours are available through the Office of Visitor Relations to explore campus history and traditions. (Mizzou Parking)
attractions.nearby: Downtown Columbia (The District), Stephens Lake Park, State Historical Society of Missouri (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Village of Hartsburg
attractions.description: Hartsburg is a historic village in southern Boone County, Missouri, known as the 'Pumpkin Capital of Missouri' and a key stop along the Katy Trail. Situated in the Missouri River bottoms, it offers a peaceful, rural atmosphere with a population of approximately 133 residents (Wikipedia, Visit Columbia MO).

Hackman Building, listed on the National Register of Historic Places (HMdb)', 'Scenic proximity to the Missouri River and Hart Creek Conservation Area (Missouri Department of Conservation)', 'Quiet, idyllic community featuring local wineries and small-town eateries (YouTube)']

attractions.name: Weldon Spring Site Interpretive Center
attractions.description: The Weldon Spring Site Interpretive Center highlights the history of a former World War II explosives manufacturing and Cold War uranium processing facility. It details the site's extensive environmental remediation and ongoing long-term stewardship through interactive exhibits and educational programs. (Energy.gov)
attractions.hours: Monday to Friday: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.; Saturday and Sunday: 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. (Energy.gov)
attractions.days_open: Open Daily (Monday-Sunday) except federal holidays, New Year’s Day, Easter, and Christmas Day (Energy.gov)
attractions.admission: Free (Energy.gov)
highlights: 'Stairs leading to the top of a 75-foot-tall disposal cell with panoramic views ([Energy.gov)', '150-acre Howell Prairie featuring a wide variety of native plants and wildlife (Energy.gov)', 'Exhibit gallery detailing World War II and Cold War history and cleanup efforts (Energy.gov)', 'Direct access to the 6-mile Hamburg Trail and connections to the Katy Trail (Energy.gov)', 'Native plant garden with paths and benches for visitors (Energy.gov)', 'Educational programs and tours for students and the public (Energy.gov)']
attractions.tips: Visit on a clear day to see the Gateway Arch from the top of the disposal cell. Wear comfortable shoes for climbing the 75-foot mound and exploring the gravel trails. (99% Invisible, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: August A. Busch Memorial Conservation Area, Katy Trail State Park, St. Charles Historic District (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Westminster College
attractions.description: Westminster College is a private liberal arts college in Fulton, Missouri, founded in 1851 as Fulton College (Wikipedia)). It is globally recognized as the location where Winston Churchill delivered his historic 1946 "Iron Curtain" speech and serves as the home to America's National Churchill Museum (Westminster College).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM (National Churchill Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults: $16, Seniors (60+): $13, Teens (12-18) & College Students: $9, Children (11 and under): Free, Members/Westminster Faculty, Staff, Students: Free (National Churchill Museum)
highlights: "America's National Churchill Museum ([National Churchill Museum)", 'The Columns, the remains of the original 1854 Westminster Hall (Wikipedia))', 'Church of St. Mary the Virgin, Aldermanbury, a reconstructed 17th-century London church (National Churchill Museum)', "'Breakthrough' sculpture created from eight sections of the original Berlin Wall (Westminster College)", "Historic Gymnasium, the site of Churchill's famous speech and a National Historic Landmark (Wikipedia))"]
attractions.tips: Visitors should allow at least 90 minutes to explore the museum exhibits and the reconstructed Wren church (National Churchill Museum). Be sure to view the Berlin Wall sections and the iconic Columns, which are centerpieces of campus tradition (Westminster College).

Today, it houses the Bagnell Dam History Museum, the Lake of the Ozarks Visitor's Center, and the Lake Area Chamber of Commerce, offering visitors an interactive look at the region's rich history and stunning lake views. Official Website, Wikipedia
